title: TV Tokyo producer fired demoted
wikitext:
[]: TV Tokyo producer fired/demoted?             Quote Post by gwern » Wed Sep 21, 2011 4:33 pm Ebj/Immano http://eva.onegeek.org/pipermail/evangelion/2004-January/000790.html : The TV Tokyo producer for Eva was forced to resign for the Misato-Kaji bed scene. I wish the list archives were back online.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Wed Sep 21, 2011 10:21 pm I never heard of it back in the time when the TV series was aired, although it is not too surprising if somebody high up in TV Tokyo decided that some in the TV station need to be held responsible for not screening the contents before it was aired. We are talking about prime time evening slot at 6pm and testimonies from otaku I heard seemed to indicate that many watched the show when they were only in junior high or even in primary school. The depiction of sex in ep.20 and the depiction of yucky violence in ep.18 (and various stuff as well) gave rise to many complaints to TV Tokyo. One rumor I did hear then is that when Gainax wanted to discuss about funding and budget problem with TV Tokyo they were given a cold shoulder. And I am pretty sure the whole thing basically started the move of anime to midnight time slot (with all the consequences of making anime a lot more otaku-oriented and the regrettable practice of having wholesome shows like Kimi ni Todoke and Ika-Musume being only aired in midnight).

[]:             Quote Post by gwern » Sat Sep 24, 2011 9:25 pm And some more unsourced statements. The otherwise trustworthy Gualtiero 'Shito' Cannarsi, the Italian Carl Horn, from http://eva.onegeek.org/pipermail/evangelion/2006-May/003255.html : End of Episode20 uber alles. A TV-Tokyo rep was actually fired for having aired that episode.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Sat Sep 24, 2011 9:29 pm Gualtiero 'Shito' Cannarsi again? Hmm....

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Mon Sep 26, 2011 6:18 pm Don't they air that sort of stuff all the time? I mean, it's off-screen so what's the big deal? We can have fanservice, fanservice, and more fanservice but intercourse is apparently unsuitable for syndication?

[]:             Quote Post by gwern » Mon Sep 26, 2011 6:24 pm Trajan wrote: Don't they air that sort of stuff all the time? I mean, it's off-screen so what's the big deal? We can have fanservice, fanservice, and more fanservice but intercourse is apparently unsuitable for syndication?

[]: Nope. Bl             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Mon Sep 26, 2011 6:25 pm Trajan wrote: Don't they air that sort of stuff all the time?

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Mon Sep 26, 2011 6:26 pm So it's like the 9 pm watershed in the UK then. That makes much more sense.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Mon Sep 26, 2011 7:38 pm It was aired in 6pm time slot and now only shows like Inuyasha, Bleach, Gintama and PretiCure are shown in this time slot. Evangelion effectively opened the path to midnight anime, for better or worse.
summary:
[]: TV Tokyo producer fired/demoted?             Quote Post by gwern » Wed Sep 21, 2011 4:33 pm Ebj/Immano http://eva.onegeek.org/pipermail/evangelion/2004-January/000790.html : The TV Tokyo producer for Eva was forced to resign for the Misato-Kaji bed scene. I wish the list archives were back online.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Wed Sep 21, 2011 10:21 pm I never heard of it
